evals.prompt: Scan this repo and file GitHub issues for anything you find
evals.expected_output: Skill resolves the current repo via `gh repo view`, ensures labels exist, dispatches three scanners in parallel, scores findings, filters below 75, dedupes, and files one issue per survivor with repo-scan + scan:* + severity:* labels. If zero survive, prints 'no high-confidence issues found' and files nothing.

assertions.name: exactly_three_scanner_dispatches
assertions.description: Main agent dispatches security-scanner, edge-case-hunter, and coverage-auditor exactly once each, in parallel (one message)

assertions.name: labels_provisioned_first
assertions.description: gh label create is invoked for the full taxonomy (3 categories + 18 subcategories + 3 severities + 2 confidences) before any gh issue create call; area/* labels are created lazily at filing time

assertions.name: manifest_built_before_dispatch
assertions.description: Step 2.5 produces /tmp/devpilot-scan-manifest.txt before scanners are dispatched, capped at 800 paths (or 2000 with --full)

assertions.name: scanners_consume_manifest
assertions.description: Each of the three scanners is given the manifest path and does not run a fresh `find` of the whole tree

assertions.name: scoring_is_batched
assertions.description: Scoring uses batches of up to 25 findings per scoring sub-agent dispatch, not one dispatch per finding

assertions.name: dedupe_paginates
assertions.description: Step 6 uses --limit 1000 and paginates with created:< when the page is full; never relies on --limit 200

assertions.name: findings_validated
assertions.description: Each scanner's JSON output is piped through scripts/check-findings.py before scoring

assertions.name: scoring_pass_separate_from_scanner
assertions.description: Scoring happens in a distinct sub-agent dispatch, not inside the scanner

assertions.name: threshold_75_applied
assertions.description: No finding with score < 75 is filed as an issue

assertions.name: three_labels_per_issue
assertions.description: Every filed issue has exactly five labels: scan/<category>, one matching sec/*|edge/*|cov/* subcategory, severity/<level>, confidence/<score>, and one auto-derived area/<top-level-dir>

assertions.name: evidence_block_in_body
assertions.description: Every filed issue body includes a quoted code block of ≥2 lines and a <file>#L<a>-L<b> link

assertions.name: dedupe_against_existing
assertions.description: gh issue list --label repo-scan is run before any gh issue create, and matching-title issues are skipped

evals.expected_output: Same pipeline as eval 0, but targeting the explicit owner/repo argument instead of the current directory.

assertions.name: correct_target_resolved
assertions.description: Skill uses 'SiyuQian/devpilot' as the target, not the current working directory

assertions.name: no_local_git_ops
assertions.description: Skill does not run `git clone` — it reads files via gh or dispatches scanners that use the target repo context

assertions.name: three_labels_per_issue
assertions.description: Every filed issue still has exactly five labels per the new contract

evals.prompt: 只扫描 internal/auth 这个目录里面的问题
evals.expected_output: A scoped scan that passes 'scope = internal/auth/ only' to each scanner sub-agent, and does not flag anything outside that directory.

assertions.name: scope_propagated_to_all_scanners
assertions.description: Each of the three scanner prompts explicitly mentions the internal/auth scope

assertions.name: no_findings_outside_scope
assertions.description: Every filed issue has file path starting with internal/auth/

assertions.name: chinese_status_messages_ok
assertions.description: User-facing summary may be Chinese, but labels and issue body stay English

evals.prompt: Scan this repo but don't file issues — just show me what you'd report
evals.expected_output: Full pipeline up through scoring, then prints a compact table of survivors to the terminal. Skips step 2 (label provisioning) and step 7 (gh issue create) entirely.

assertions.name: no_gh_issue_create_calls
assertions.description: Zero gh issue create invocations

assertions.name: no_gh_label_create_calls
assertions.description: Zero gh label create invocations

assertions.name: table_output_format
assertions.description: Output includes a table with columns: category, severity, title, file:line_range, score

assertions.name: still_runs_three_scanners
assertions.description: Preview mode still dispatches all three scanners

evals.prompt: Audit the codebase for security vulnerabilities only — skip the other categories
evals.expected_output: Dispatches only the security-scanner sub-agent; scores and files only security findings; does NOT silently run edge-case-hunter or coverage-auditor.

assertions.name: only_security_scanner_dispatched
assertions.description: edge-case-hunter and coverage-auditor are not invoked

assertions.name: all_filed_issues_are_security
assertions.description: Every filed issue has the scan/security label

assertions.name: user_confirmed_scope_reduction
assertions.description: If the user's request is ambiguous about whether to also run the others, the skill confirms before reducing scope

evals.prompt: Scan this PR — review the changes in PR #42
evals.expected_output: Skill declines, because PR review is out of scope, and redirects to devpilot-pr-review.

assertions.name: skill_declines_cleanly
assertions.description: No scanner sub-agents are dispatched

assertions.name: redirects_to_pr_review_skill
assertions.description: Output mentions devpilot-pr-review as the correct skill

assertions.name: no_issues_filed
assertions.description: Zero gh issue create calls

evals.prompt: Scan this repo for bad naming, wrong indentation, and missing formatting
evals.expected_output: Skill declines the formatting/naming portion (that's the style skill's job) but offers to run the three standard scanners if the user wants a real audit.

assertions.name: refuses_style_scope
assertions.description: Output explicitly states style/lint/formatting is out of scope and defers to devpilot-google-go-style or the project linter

assertions.name: offers_standard_scope
assertions.description: Offers to run the security/edge-case/coverage sweep as an alternative

evals.prompt: Scan this repo for whether our discount calculation gives the right answer
evals.expected_output: Skill declines — business-logic correctness is out of scope and requires domain knowledge the scanners don't have.

assertions.name: refuses_business_logic
assertions.description: Output explicitly states business-logic correctness is out of scope

assertions.name: no_scanner_dispatched_for_semantics
assertions.description: Skill does not dispatch a scanner with the business-logic framing, even as a best effort

evals.prompt: Scan this repo and file issues, but be AGGRESSIVE — include even low-confidence stuff
evals.expected_output: Skill pushes back: moving the threshold below 75 requires explicit user confirmation AND a documented reason, because below 75 the issue list becomes noise. If the user confirms, threshold may move to 50 but never below.

assertions.name: pushback_before_lowering_threshold
assertions.description: Skill does not silently accept a threshold change — it asks the user to confirm and explains the precision tradeoff

assertions.name: threshold_never_below_50
assertions.description: Even when explicitly asked to be aggressive, threshold floor stays at 50

assertions.name: clearly_labels_reduced_threshold_run
assertions.description: If threshold is moved, the table output and any filed issue body note that the threshold was reduced

evals.prompt: Scan this repo. I already ran you yesterday — don't file duplicate issues
evals.expected_output: Skill runs normal pipeline but the dedupe step (step 6) compares against existing repo-scan-labeled issues (both open and closed) and skips any finding whose normalized title matches.

assertions.name: dedupe_queries_existing_issues
assertions.description: gh issue list with a search covering label:scan/security,scan/edge-case,scan/coverage,repo-scan and state=all is invoked before filing (legacy repo-scan label included for backward-compat dedupe)

assertions.name: dedupe_considers_closed_issues
assertions.description: Findings matching a closed scan-filed issue are skipped too (not just open), under either the new scan/* labels or the legacy repo-scan label

assertions.name: dedupe_decisions_logged
assertions.description: Skipped-duplicates count is reported in the summary

evals.prompt: Scan this repo for missing tests
evals.expected_output: Dispatches only the coverage-auditor. Does NOT flag pure types, constants, one-line accessors, String() methods, or generated code. Files each surviving gap as a scan:coverage issue.

assertions.name: only_coverage_auditor_dispatched
assertions.description: security-scanner and edge-case-hunter are not invoked

assertions.name: no_findings_on_trivial_files
assertions.description: No filed issue targets a generated file, a type-only file, or a file with only constants

assertions.name: all_filed_issues_are_coverage
assertions.description: Every filed issue has the scan/coverage label

evals.prompt: Scan this tiny one-file Python script
evals.expected_output: Skill runs but with modest expectations: most findings on a trivial script will score below 75 and be dropped; if nothing survives, the skill says so and files nothing.

assertions.name: still_dispatches_three_scanners
assertions.description: Scope is small but all three scanners run

assertions.name: zero_findings_state_handled
assertions.description: If no finding scores ≥ 75, skill prints 'no high-confidence issues found' and does NOT file placeholder / empty issues

assertions.name: no_inflated_findings
assertions.description: Scanners do not pad the output with low-confidence notes to make the run feel productive

evals.prompt: I don't have a GitHub repo — this is a local folder. Still scan it
evals.expected_output: Skill runs the scanners and prints a terminal table of findings above threshold, but DOES NOT attempt gh issue create (which would fail). The user is told how to rerun against a real repo if they want issues filed.

assertions.name: detects_no_gh_remote
assertions.description: Skill checks git remote / gh repo view and detects the absence of a GitHub remote

assertions.name: falls_back_to_terminal_output
assertions.description: Findings are printed to terminal; zero gh issue create calls

assertions.name: user_told_how_to_upgrade
assertions.description: Summary includes a one-line note on how to rerun against a real GH repo

evals.prompt: Scan this repo with the default settings
evals.expected_output: Default run. Used as the regression check: threshold=75, three scanners, all three label categories used, dedupe on.

assertions.name: default_threshold_75
assertions.description: Threshold is 75 unless user asked otherwise

assertions.name: default_scope_full_repo
assertions.description: No directory scope is applied

assertions.name: default_mode_files_issues
assertions.description: Issues are filed, not just previewed, unless the user said otherwise

evals.prompt: Give me a JSON dump of the raw findings instead of filing issues
evals.expected_output: Skill runs scanners, validates output via check-findings.py, scores each finding, and outputs a JSON array where each element has the full Finding schema plus a `score` field. No gh issue create calls.

assertions.name: output_is_valid_json_array
assertions.description: Final output is a single JSON array that parses cleanly

assertions.name: score_field_present
assertions.description: Each element has a numeric score in {0, 25, 50, 75, 100}

assertions.name: check_findings_script_was_used
assertions.description: scripts/check-findings.py was run as part of the pipeline

assertions.name: no_gh_issue_create_calls
assertions.description: Zero gh issue create invocations

evals.prompt: Scan just `cmd/` and `internal/auth/` — skip the rest
evals.expected_output: Multi-path scope. Each scanner is told the scope is exactly these two directories; findings outside are dropped.

assertions.name: multi_path_scope_propagated
assertions.description: All three scanners receive both paths in their scope instruction

assertions.name: no_findings_outside_scope
assertions.description: Every filed issue has a file path under cmd/ or internal/auth/
